name: Crash Multiplier Sequences: A 1,000,000 Round Monte Carlo Simulation Study
description: A synthetic longitudinal time-series dataset containing 1,000,000 sequentially simulated rounds modeled via a four-tier Piecewise Uniform Distribution. Features engineered include short-term historical lags, micro-rolling averages, and look-ahead-bias-free macro rolling mean and standard deviation windows. Purpose-built for econometric testing, risk modeling, and studying stochastic behavior in heavy-tailed random processes.

variableMeasured.name: round_id
variableMeasured.description: Sequential integer increment acting as the unique primary key for each simulation state.

variableMeasured.name: timestamp
variableMeasured.description: ISO-8601 pseudo-chronological marker tracking sequential increments of delta t = 15 seconds.

variableMeasured.name: session_id
variableMeasured.description: Categorical sliding block separator aggregating blocks of exactly 1,000 rounds per batch.

variableMeasured.name: crash_point
variableMeasured.description: Target continuous stochastic variable generated by a four-bracket piecewise uniform random interval capped at 500.00x.

variableMeasured.name: previous_crash_1
variableMeasured.description: Lag-1 short-term memory marker recording the immediate past realization value (X_t-1).

variableMeasured.name: previous_crash_5_avg
variableMeasured.description: Arithmetic short-term rolling mean of the 5 preceding observations. Pre-burn-in rows default to null (NaN).

variableMeasured.name: rolling_mean_20
variableMeasured.description: Leakage-free baseline rolling mean calculated strictly over the historical window of 20 elements (X_t-20 to X_t-1).

variableMeasured.name: rolling_std_20
variableMeasured.description: Unbiased local sample standard deviation calculated over the 20 historical lookback windows applying Bessel's Correction (ddof=1).

measurementTechnique: Monte Carlo Piecewise Random Engine Generation with Anti-Look-Ahead Feature Isolation Filter.
